title: PrognosisMeta: Browser-Based Prognostic Meta-Analysis Engine
author: Mahmood Ahmad
summary: A 29,770-line JavaScript engine achieves 93.3% exact match with R metafor for prognostic meta-analysis.
body: Can a browser-based application implement publication-quality prognostic meta-analysis methods validated against R metafor? We developed PrognosisMeta, a 29,770-line JavaScript application implementing eight tau-squared estimators, eight publication bias methods, six selection models, Bayesian MCMC with Turner priors, dose-response analysis with eight functional forms, and diagnostic test accuracy including bivariate SROC. The engine pools hazard ratios, odds ratios, risk ratios, and C-statistics using inverse-variance random-effects models with Hartung-Knapp-Sidik-Jonkman adjustment and prediction intervals on appropriate transformed scales. Validation against R metafor across three datasets showed 93.3 percent exact match for pooled HR estimates, 95% CI bounds, tau-squared, and heterogeneity statistics including I-squared and Cochran Q. Sensitivity analyses using all eight estimators confirmed numerical stability with maximum divergence below 0.001 across every test configuration. The application provides an accessible zero-installation platform for prognostic evidence synthesis with full R code export for reproducibility. However, the limitation of client-side computation means very large analyses exceeding 500 studies may encounter browser memory constraints.

sentences.text: Can a browser-based application implement publication-quality prognostic meta-analysis methods validated against R metafor?

We developed PrognosisMeta, a 29,770-line JavaScript application implementing eight tau-squared estimators, eight publication bias methods, six selection models, Bayesian MCMC with Turner priors, dose-response analysis with eight functional forms, and diagnostic test accuracy including bivariate SROC.

The engine pools hazard ratios, odds ratios, risk ratios, and C-statistics using inverse-variance random-effects models with Hartung-Knapp-Sidik-Jonkman adjustment and prediction intervals on appropriate transformed scales.
Validation against R metafor across three datasets showed 93.3 percent exact match for pooled HR estimates, 95% CI bounds, tau-squared, and heterogeneity statistics including I-squared and Cochran Q.
Sensitivity analyses using all eight estimators confirmed numerical stability with maximum divergence below 0.001 across every test configuration.
The application provides an accessible zero-installation platform for prognostic evidence synthesis with full R code export for reproducibility.
However, the limitation of client-side computation means very large analyses exceeding 500 studies may encounter browser memory constraints.

notes.data: Three benchmark datasets, R metafor v4.8.0 reference values

references: Crippa A, Orsini N. Dose-response meta-analysis of differences in means. BMC Med Res Methodol. 2016;16:91.
Greenland S, Longnecker MP. Methods for trend estimation from summarized dose-response data, with applications to meta-analysis. Am J Epidemiol. 1992;135(11):1301-1309.
Borenstein M, Hedges LV, Higgins JPT, Rothstein HR. Introduction to Meta-Analysis. 2nd ed. Wiley; 2021.
